2) 轉貼相關新聞 (有文宣更好，但暫時未有)
e.g.
Hong Kong airline Cathay Dragon fires flight attendant union chief amid pressure from China

行動二: 一人一email 寄去國際航空組織
由陳雲提出
隔離post oggy巴打打左範本
https://lihkg.com/thread/1493700/page/24
Muuu 巴打修正
https://lihkg.com/thread/1497471/page/9

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing an email to express my concern for Cathay Pacific Airways (IATA code:CX) is political repressed by Chinese authority.

Hong Kong has been under spotlight in the past two months for protesting against a proposed legislation, the Fugitive Offenders Ordinance, which potentially threatens local citizens and foreign travelers who are suspected of committing offence listed in this ordinance by Chinese Government. This proposed legislation allows transferring of any suspects to Mainland China.

Over two million of protestors in Hong Kong walked on the streets on demonstration and expressed their demands peacefully. Protestors also tried different measures such as strikes for voicing their opinions out. Cathay's crucial role came into focus much more recently. More than a thousand of its employees participated in a strike that forced the airline to cancel over 150 flights. Days later, Chinese Government forced Cathay Pacific CEO to give names of staff joining Hong Kong protest with a threat that does not allowing planes of Cathay Pacific flies over China. Yet CEO refused to do that. Cathay is solely grappling with threats from Chinese Government. As a result, Cathay had terminated two pilots in association with activities related to the protests and the company announced that CEO Rupert Hogg and Chief Commercial Officer Paul Loo were out on 16th Aug.

Today (23-Aug), president of Union of Cathay Dragon Cabin Crew, Rebecca Sy has been fired/forced to resign. Pro-Democracy Staff of Cathay Group are bearing a lot pressure from China government and the management board has been takeover by Pro-China members.

Source
https://twitter.com/hashtag/cathay

According to the article 27 in Hong Kong Basic Law, Hong Kong residents shall have freedom of speech, of the press and of publication; freedom of association, of assembly, of procession and of demonstration; and the right and freedom to form and join trade unions, and to strike. Hong Kong and Chinese government ignored that article which entitles all Hong Kong citizens to have right to participate strikes. More terribly, Cathay Pacific continuous looking for staff who took part in protest and dismiss them under the threat of Chinese Government.

Aviation brings benefits to national and global economies. None of aviation companies should be threatened due to political reasons. IATA, as the largest representation of aviation industry, should stand out with the employees of Cathay Pacific. We sincerely wish you to condemn China’s interference that violates International Air Service Transit Agreement.

Best wishes,
XXXXXXXX

行動二: 一人一email 寄去國際航空組織
由陳雲提出
隔離post oggy巴打打左範本

Muuu 巴打修正
https://lihkg.com/thread/1497471/page/9

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing an email to express my concern for Cathay Pacific Airways (IATA code:CX) is political repressed by Chinese authority.

Hong Kong has been under spotlight in the past two months for protesting against a proposed legislation, the Fugitive Offenders Ordinance, which potentially threatens local citizens and foreign travelers who are suspected of committing offence listed in this ordinance by Chinese Government. This proposed legislation allows transferring of any suspects to Mainland China.

Over two million of protestors in Hong Kong walked on the streets on demonstration and expressed their demands peacefully. Protestors also tried different measures such as strikes for voicing their opinions out. Cathay's crucial role came into focus much more recently. More than a thousand of its employees participated in a strike that forced the airline to cancel over 150 flights. Days later, Chinese Government forced Cathay Pacific CEO to give names of staff joining Hong Kong protest with a threat that does not allowing planes of Cathay Pacific flies over China. Yet CEO refused to do that. Cathay is solely grappling with threats from Chinese Government. As a result, Cathay had terminated two pilots in association with activities related to the protests and the company announced that CEO Rupert Hogg and Chief Commercial Officer Paul Loo were out on 16th Aug.

Today (23-Aug), president of Union of Cathay Dragon Cabin Crew, Rebecca Sy has been fired/forced to resign. Pro-Democracy Staff of Cathay Group are bearing a lot pressure from China government and the management board has been takeover by Pro-China members.

Source
https://twitter.com/hashtag/cathay

According to the article 27 in Hong Kong Basic Law, Hong Kong residents shall have freedom of speech, of the press and of publication; freedom of association, of assembly, of procession and of demonstration; and the right and freedom to form and join trade unions, and to strike. Hong Kong and Chinese government ignored that article which entitles all Hong Kong citizens to have right to participate strikes. More terribly, Cathay Pacific continuous looking for staff who took part in protest and dismiss them under the threat of Chinese Government.

Aviation brings benefits to national and global economies. None of aviation companies should be threatened due to political reasons. IATA, as the largest representation of aviation industry, should stand out with the employees of Cathay Pacific. We sincerely wish you to condemn China’s interference that violates International Air Service Transit Agreement.

Best wishes,
XXXXXXXX

做實事捍衛國泰懶人包: 今晚11點twitter + email 國際航空組織 + 聯署美國貿易代表處
https://lih.kg/1497890
- 分享自 LIHKG 討論區

二、美國貿易代表--->防止紅色企業滲透
Send to ustr_scheduling@ustr.eop.gov

United States Trade Representative
Attn: Mr Robert Lighthizer

Dear Mr Lighthizer,

I am writing to express my concern for the issue of political suppression of Cathay Pacific and interference in free market by Chinese government.

Hong Kong has been under spotlight in the past two months for protesting against a proposed legislation, the Fugitive Offenders Ordinance, which potentially threatens local citizens and foreign travelers who are suspected of committing offence listed in this ordinance by Chinese Government. This proposed legislation was established to transfer of any suspects to Mainland China.

Over two million of protestors in Hong Kong walked on the streets on demonstration and expressed their demands peacefully. Protestors also tried different measures such as strikes for voicing their opinions out. Cathay's crucial role came into focus much more recently. More than a thousand of its employees participated in a strike that forced the airline to cancel over 150 flights. Days later, according to news reports, Chinese Government forced Cathay Pacific CEO to give names of staff joining Hong Kong protest with a threat that does not allowing Cathay's planes of Cathay Pacific flies over China. Yet CEO refused giving out names. Cathay eventually terminated two pilots in association with activities related to the protests and the company announced that CEO Rupert Hogg and Chief Commercial Officer Paul Loo were out on 16th Aug. But the issue does not come to the end. It is assumed that more and more staff will be in reprisal for participating in the protest. Also, we can see that Cathay is solely grappling with threats from Chinese Government.

According to the article 27 in Hong Kong Basic Law, Hong Kong residents shall have freedom of speech, of the press and of publication; freedom of association, of assembly, of procession and of demonstration; and the right and freedom to form and join trade unions, and to strike. Hong Kong and Chinese government ignored that article which entitles all Hong Kong citizens to have right to participate strikes.

Cathay Pacific kowtowed to Chinese government will not likely be an anomaly. In fact, Chinese government is already increasing its efforts to control companies and pressure them to conform to the party line. For instance, Liu Wen, a Chinese model, resigned as a brand ambassador for Coach in the mid of August after it labelled Taiwan and Hong Kong as countries on T-shirts. The priority number one for Chinese government is maintaining party control therefore they go to stake for it by practicing at encouraging outcries on social media against slights, consumer boycotts, and more terribly, threaten to terminate business relations to China. In the case of Cathay Pacific, It was threatened to block flights over the mainland which meant China violated international air transit agreement with its tyrannical power.

It is dangerous sign, to local and foreign business in Hong Kong, which revealed a fact that China’s political intervention on foreign private enterprises. If Chinese government succeeds in repressing local and foreign enterprises rooted in Hong Kong, more competitors from China would harm local and foreign enterprises’ profit. It is possible that those China-back companies request to draft a policy to pander to Chinese companies but loss local and foreign enterprises’ profit in Legislation Council(similar Congress in the USA). It could be that bad money drives out good. More terribly, China expands their power globally by infiltrating other foreign enterprises with purchasing shares of that company. It would harm global business development.

We sincerely request you, Mr Lighthizer, to include this concern in the next Sino-US trade negotiation for all local and foreign enterprises in Hong Kong to have enough protection and independence in the free market.

Thank you for reading
Best wishes,
A Hong Kong citizen

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing an email to express my concern for Cathay Pacific Airways (IATA code:CX) is political repressed by Chinese authority.

Hong Kong has been under spotlight in the past two months for protesting against a proposed legislation, the Fugitive Offenders Ordinance, which potentially threatens local citizens and foreign travelers who are suspected of committing offence listed in this ordinance by Chinese Government. This proposed legislation allows transferring of any suspects to Mainland China.

Over two million of protestors in Hong Kong walked on the streets on demonstration and expressed their demands peacefully. Protestors also tried different measures such as strikes for voicing their opinions out. Cathay's crucial role came into focus much more recently. More than a thousand of its employees participated in a strike that forced the airline to cancel over 150 flights. Days later, Chinese Government forced Cathay Pacific CEO to give names of staff joining Hong Kong protest with a threat that does not allowing planes of Cathay Pacific flies over China. Yet CEO refused to do that. Cathay is solely grappling with threats from Chinese Government. As a result, Cathay had terminated two pilots in association with activities related to the protests and the company announced that CEO Rupert Hogg and Chief Commercial Officer Paul Loo were out on 16th Aug.

Today (23-Aug), president of Union of Cathay Dragon Cabin Crew, Rebecca Sy has been fired/forced to resign. Pro-Democracy Staff of Cathay Group are bearing a lot pressure from China government and the management board has been takeover by Pro-China members.

Source
https://twitter.com/hashtag/cathay

According to the article 27 in Hong Kong Basic Law, Hong Kong residents shall have freedom of speech, of the press and of publication; freedom of association, of assembly, of procession and of demonstration; and the right and freedom to form and join trade unions, and to strike. Hong Kong and Chinese government ignored that article which entitles all Hong Kong citizens to have right to participate strikes. More terribly, Cathay Pacific continuous looking for staff who took part in protest and dismiss them under the threat of Chinese Government.

Aviation brings benefits to national and global economies. None of aviation companies should be threatened due to political reasons. IATA, as the largest representation of aviation industry, should stand out with the employees of Cathay Pacific. We sincerely wish you to condemn China’s interference that violates International Air Service Transit Agreement.

Best wishes,
XXXXXXXX

係針對ICAO嘅

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my concerns on Cathay Pacific Airways (IATA code: CX), which is currently under political pressure from the Chinese authority.

In the past two months, Hong Kong has been under the spotlight for protesting against proposed legislation, the Fugitive Offenders Ordinance, which will potentially threaten not only local citizens but foreign travelers. Once one is suspected of committing an offence listed in this ordinance by the Chinese Government, the proposed legislation allows transferring of any suspects to Mainland China without any judicial procedures and judgments, which strongly damage the common law system and the rule of law in Hong Kong, especially the principle of presumption of innocence.

Other than the well-known two-millions demonstration, protestors also exerted different means to express their opinion and ask the government to respond the public demands, including strikes, non-cooperation actions, human chain as so. During the movement, Cathay Pacific has played a crucial role. More than a thousand of its employees participated in a general strike to urge the government to face the public and the public demands.

Yet, after the general strike, the Civil Aviation Administration of China, in the name of security concerns, forced Cathay Pacific CEO Rupert Hogg to submit a list of those participated in protests and published speech against HKSAR and China, otherwise their flights will be prohibited to fly over China's airspace. Other than the official prohibition of access to China's airspace, official media like Global Times and state enterprises/banks have criticized Cathay Pacific for not enough patriotism and downgraded Cathay's credit rating. Although Rupert Hogg and Chief Commercial Officer Paul Loo decided to bear full responsibility and resign to respond the demands from China on 16th Aug, the suppression and purge have still not yet come to an end. For instance, Cathay Pacific terminated two pilots in association with activities related to the protests, one pilot with a charge of riot but not yet judged as guilty, a pilot who only said "Hongkoners Stand Strong" during landing announcement, and a flight attendant who is also the chair of union due to speeches supporting protests on social media platform.

According to the article 27 in Hong Kong Basic Law, Hong Kong residents shall have freedom of speech, of the press and of publication; freedom of association, of assembly, of procession and of demonstration; and the right and freedom to form and join trade unions, and to strike. Hong Kong and Chinese government ignored that articles which entitle all Hong Kong citizens to have the right to strikes. More terribly, Cathay Pacific is continuously searching for staff who took part in protests and dismiss them under the threat of the Chinese Government.

It is not an internal issue but an international alert. Once the Chinese government succeed to have full control of Cathay Pacific and starts a precedent, it means that China could easily intervene and manipulate business decisions and infringe the rights and safety of foreign aviation-related practitioners.

To sustain the prosperity of aviation sector which brings enormous benefits to national and global economies, ICAO shall take immediate action against China who attempted to violate the international conventions and rules, and intervene the business decision, thus the liberal market economy system. No aviation companies shall be threatened by political suppression. ICAO, as the largest representation and international authority of the aviation industry, should stand with the employees of Cathay Pacific in response to China's brutal intervention on the international aviation sector.

We sincerely wish you to not only condemn China’s interference that violates International Air Service Transit Agreement, but also take immediate and substantive actions over China's misconduct.

Best wishes,
XXXXXXXX
